commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft with compatibility/auth-provider/availability risk needs human acceptance and exact-head proof rather than an automated repair lane.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain regression was found in the sampled diff; the security-relevant changes harden metadata/auth/approval boundaries, with compatibility risk tracked separately. (sha=ba62276788c2d978805952df03e7114a45397f9b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens plugin, provider, Codex app-server, SDK, channel/startup, and outbound action metadata/schema readers with fuzz coverage so hostile or unreadable synthetic data is bounded instead of raw-crashing.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has source-level raw JSON.stringify, Object.entries, and direct property-access paths that can throw on circular, BigInt, or getter-hostile plugin/provider/schema metadata, and the PR body reports direct pre/post repros for those classes.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft with compatibility/auth-provider/availability risk needs human acceptance and exact-head proof rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, SDK helper APIs, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent provider schema diagnostics, or outbound message action handling.
- Kept tests synthetic: current fixtures use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest slices guard provider tool descriptors and embedded-agent provider tool diagnostics: unreadable `name`, unreadable `parameters`, normalize/inspect hook throws, unreadable thrown values, unreadable diagnostic `violations`, and unreadable tool names now omit, diagnose, or log bounded warnings instead of raw-crashing schema normalization/diagnostics.
- Earlier slices harden outbound structured attachment params, outbound JSON params (`presentation`, `delivery`, `interactive`), `runMessageAction` input param copying, exported message action normalization/target-spec helpers, exported message action threading controls/writes, native approval routing, provider wizard metadata, manifest/channel metadata, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema quarantine.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action metadata can crash or widen decisions before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, approval forwarding, message action validation, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, or Codex app-server handling complete.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; broad remote validation on direct AWS Crabbox Linux runners because Blacksmith Testbox auth was unavailable in this shell.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Direct pre/post `tsx` repros for embedded-agent provider normalize hook throws and unreadable provider diagnostics.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repros for unreadable synthetic provider tool descriptors: unreadable `parameters`, unreadable `name`, and own-key traps during normalized tool copy no longer throw raw errors.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repros for synthetic message action threading writes, unreadable threading controls, message action normalization params, runner params, provider wizard metadata, manifest channel metadata, native approval approver iterators, shared action params, structured attachments, and JSON action params.
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tool-schema-runtime-fuzz-efc93 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.threading.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- Earlier focused batches for outbound normalization/params, shared action params, provider auth/onboarding, manifest/tool availability, channel/config fixtures, Codex image payloads, and fuzz guard type/lint drift.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` and `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` on the latest touched file sets.
- `git diff --check`
- Added-line scrub for reported plugin names, local paths, and private network literals.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix:

- Latest local autoreview on the embedded-agent provider diagnostics slice first found the guarded `formatError` fallback could still throw on hostile thrown values; that was fixed, and the rerun reported no accepted/actionable findings.
- Latest focused proof after rebase to `efc93bf282`: `tool-schema-runtime.test.ts` passed 2 files / 12 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- Provider-tool descriptor slice proof: `provider-tools.test.ts` passed 1 file / 15 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed.
- Message threading writes slice proof: `message-action-runner.threading.test.ts` passed 1 file / 24 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed.
- `origin/main` advanced repeatedly with zero touched-path overlap through `1901b832eb`, `3a20a0cd4f`, `5cccfe1c17`, `c903b271cf`, `091e15139b`, `61c538e2fc`, `8bd4736f03`, `f7b0b5429e`, `7bf100b028`, `ca41fa293f`, `73cf516def`, and `efc93bf282`; the branch rebased cleanly through those tips.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate after the provider-wizard slice: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_991a8c29acdf`, run `run_41811346ac17`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 on base `8e806e9125`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate for the branch: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_52b1fe398bd3`, run `run_c85914033aaa`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 over core, coreTests, extensions, and extensionTests.
- Current branch head after rebase/push: `ba62276788c2d978805952df03e7114a45397f9b`.
- Latest rebase/proof base: `efc93bf28221bcc7955b13daccac86c612675148`.
- PR target base observed after body readback: `c8f2bbf76ddcc0356e0929dbd5a13e14269105c4` (newer than the latest proof base; rebase pending for the next work slice).
- Current added-line scrub: no reported plugin name, local path, or private network literal in the branch diff.

Observed result after fix:

- Hostile synthetic config/schema/provider/manifest/native-approval/message-action inputs are bounded at the relevant owner boundary instead of throwing raw getter/enumeration/serialization/spread errors.
- Embedded-agent provider schema normalization now keeps original tools when provider hooks throw, and diagnostics logging stays bounded when inspect hooks or diagnostic/tool metadata are hostile.
- Provider tool-schema compatibility traversal now guards descriptor reads and normalized descriptor copies before inspecting provider-specific schema keywords.
- Message action normalization, threading controls, threading writes, JSON params, structured attachments, and runner param copies now fail closed at the relevant boundary instead of raw-crashing on synthetic accessors.
- Provider wizard setup choices, gateway startup manifest planning, native approval routing, manifest tool availability, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema-quarantine fixtures retain their previously proven behavior.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because `blacksmith auth login` is required in this shell; direct AWS Crabbox was used for broad remote gates.
- The latest broad Crabbox gate predates the newest outbound runner/normalization/threading/provider-tool/runtime-diagnostics slices; those slices have focused local proof and local autoreview.
- GitHub target `main` advanced to `c8f2bbf76d` after the latest rebase/proof/push; no proof has been rerun against that newer tip yet.
- GitHub PR checks are queued/running on the pushed head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new config surface. Existing malformed/unreadable config, manifest, provider, tool descriptor, diagnostic, or action inputs now fail closed or resolve as absent at bounded reads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed or unreadable tool/provider/plugin/native-approval/action metadata is rejected, omitted, or quarantined earlier instead of crashing runtime paths or broadening access.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict sanitation could omit a valid provider tool descriptor, diagnostic payload, or outbound action parameter.

How is that risk mitigated?

The changes preserve readable sibling entries, fail closed only for route/control/identity/idempotency-style action params in the newest outbound slices, keep unreadable payload-only fields as absent, preserve numeric alias behavior, and use focused regressions plus broad AWS `check:changed` gates from earlier branch slices.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the draft PR at head `ba62276788c2d978805952df03e7114a45397f9b`, or rebase once more if reviewer/CI requires the current `main` tip.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI is queued/running after the force-push. No local actionable autoreview findings remain. GitHub target `main` advanced after this push/readback, so the next work slice should start by rebasing from `c8f2bbf76d` or newer.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings were addressed, including Codex URL-backed image-block preservation, provider wizard receiver handling, native approval false-positive target matching, shared param-reader silent truncation, message action `topLevel`/`idempotencyKey` fail-closed handling, numeric legacy-target preservation, guarded message action threading controls, guarded message action threading writes, guarded provider tool descriptors, and guarded embedded-agent provider diagnostics.
target.author: vincentkoc
